inline_comments.message: doenst this have to go in /var/opt/ONAP/demo or is later copied/linked into that path ?
Could you set this and following repositories as build arg, so that one could easily build docker image from modified repositories?

not 100% sure I get your point

heatbridge , python-testing-utils, testuite and demo are needed, there are not optional to build the image used by the robot pod
you want to get the possibility to customize each component using different tags provided as argument?
so add

ARG HEATBRIDGE_TAG=master
ARG PYTHON_TESTING_UTILS_TAG=master
....

and use these tags here

so you shall be able to buil adding --build-arg HEATBRIDGE_TAG=my_customized_tag

or do you have something different in mind?

Sorry for not being clear. I've meant adding parameters (with defaults equal to current values) for repositories url like `HEATBRIDGE_REPO=git+https://git.onap.org/testsuite/heatbridge.git`.
This would enable building robot image for local test purposes from local changes without the need of modifying Dockerfile. What I mean, is you can modify something in testsuite/demo/heatbridge repo and check modified robot container without the need of pushing it upstream (creating review). Example flow you can do is:
 > git init --bare ../loc.git
 > git remote add loc ../loc.git
 > git push loc master
 > git -C ../loc.git/ update-server-info
 > cd ../ && python3 -m http.server 8000
Serving your modified git repo locally, and later build docker image passing relevant build arg to download from there, eg: `docker build --build-arg DEMO_REPO=http://some_ip:8000/loc.git`

Hopefully I've described it better now 😊?
